class Solution {
public:
    int minimumOperationsToMakeEqual(int x, int y) {
        if (x == y) return 0;
        
        queue<pair<int, int>> q;
        unordered_set<int> visited;
        q.push({x, 0});
        visited.insert(x);
        
        while (!q.empty()) {
            auto [current, steps] = q.front();
            q.pop();
            
            if (current == y) return steps;
            
            // Generate all possible next steps
            vector<int> nextValues;
            
            // Divide by 11
            if (current % 11 == 0) nextValues.push_back(current / 11);
            // Divide by 5
            if (current % 5 == 0) nextValues.push_back(current / 5);
            // Decrement by 1
            nextValues.push_back(current - 1);
            // Increment by 1
            nextValues.push_back(current + 1);
            
            for (int next : nextValues) {
                if (next == y) {
                    return steps + 1;
                }
                // Check if next is not visited
                if (visited.find(next) == visited.end()) {
                    q.push({next, steps + 1});
                    visited.insert(next);
                }
            }
        }
        return -1; // Should not reach here as per problem statement
    }
};

#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int minimumOperationsToMakeEqual(int x, int y) {
        if (x == y) return 0;
        
        queue<pair<int, int>> q;
        unordered_set<int> visited;
        q.push({x, 0});
        visited.insert(x);
        
        while (!q.empty()) {
            auto [current, steps] = q.front();
            q.pop();
            
            if (current == y) return steps;
            
            vector<int> nextValues;
            
            if (current % 11 == 0) nextValues.push_back(current / 11);
            if (current % 5 == 0) nextValues.push_back(current / 5);
            nextValues.push_back(current - 1);
            nextValues.push_back(current + 1);
            
            for (int next : nextValues) {
                if (next == y) {
                    return steps + 1;
                }
                if (visited.find(next) == visited.end()) {
                    visited.insert(next);
                    q.push({next, steps + 1});
                }
            }
        }
        return -1; // Should not reach here
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
#include <queue>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <vector>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int minimumOperationsToMakeEqual(int x, int y) {
        if (x == y) return 0;
        
        queue<pair<int, int>> q;
        unordered_set<int> visited;
        q.push({x, 0});
        visited.insert(x);
        
        while (!q.empty()) {
            auto [current, steps] = q.front();
            q.pop();
            
            if (current == y) return steps;
            
            vector<int> nextValues;
            
            if (current % 11 == 0) nextValues.push_back(current / 11);
            if (current % 5 == 0) nextValues.push_back(current / 5);
            nextValues.push_back(current - 1);
            nextValues.push_back(current + 1);
            
            for (int next : nextValues) {
                if (next == y) return steps + 1;
                if (visited.find(next) == visited.end()) {
                    visited.insert(next);
                    q.push({next, steps + 1});
                }
            }
        }
        return -1;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses BFS to explore all possible operations (division by 11, division by 5, increment, decrement) level by level, ensuring the shortest path is found. BFS guarantees the minimum steps due to its level-order traversal. The visited set prevents redundant processing. Time and space complexity depend on the number of unique states processed,
